feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: West Nile virus detected in southern state as health officials warn residents about mosquitoes
entry.summary: Nashville detects West Nile virus in mosquitoes for the first time in 2026, prompting officials to urge residents to eliminate standing water and use DEET.

entry.title: Heat wave warning: Common medications that can increase your risk of heat illness
entry.summary: A heat wave can be dangerous for anyone, but some everyday prescription and over-the-counter medications can make it harder for the body to handle high heat, increasing the risk of dehydration, dizziness, fainting, and heat illness.

entry.title: CDC seeks source of parasitic outbreak linked to ‘explosive’ diarrhea in multiple states
entry.summary: People usually become infected from eating contaminated food or water.

entry.title: 11-year-old boy dies from rabies after waking up to bat on his face
entry.summary: A new medical report details how an 11-year-old boy died of rabies after waking up with a bat on his face while sleeping. Here's more on his death.

entry.title: ‘World’s most contagious disease’ on track for highest US cases in 35 years — doc warns: ‘We are allowing it to spread’
entry.summary: “This is a major public health warning sign,” epidemiologist Dr. Syra Madad told The Post. “The US is already close to last year’s total with about half the year still ahead.&#822…

entry.title: A Philly woman knew something was wrong. It took 4 ER visits to find her cancer
entry.summary: Medical advances mean that more people are surviving cancer. But not everyone is benefiting from those gains equally, a new report shows.

entry.title: My Boyfriend and I Signed Up for a Summer of Madness. Now Our Sex Life Is Reaping the Consequences.
entry.summary: Why did we do this?

entry.title: Tips to prevent cyclospora infection and explosive diarrhea
entry.summary: At least 170 Michiganders have contracted a parasitic gastrointestinal infection called cyclosporiasis. Here are the CDC's tips to prevent illness.

entry.title: 6-year-old girl bitten by rabid bat while playing outside her Wisconsin home, family says
entry.summary: The girl’s mother said her sons killed the bat with a homemade “Braveheart”-inspired sword. Her daughter is recovering.

entry.title: Even high amounts of coffee may lower your risk for liver disease
entry.summary: Drinking coffee may lower your risk for liver cancer and other liver disease, according to a new study.

entry.title: The Best Vegetable to Help You Poop, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Do peas help with constipation? Green peas provide fiber, fluids and magnesium to support regularity and digestive health. Learn how peas help you poop.

entry.title: After 14 Months of Chemotherapy, Girl, 5, Has One Request: 'Can We Go to the Beach?'
entry.summary: Maddie Fries underwent a brutal treatment schedule to preserve her vision after doctors discovered tumors on her optic nerves

entry.title: Canadian boy, 11, died from rabies after waking up with bat on his mouth
entry.summary: An 11-year-old Canadian boy has died from rabies after waking up with a bat on his nose and mouth.

entry.title: The Best Times to Drink Coffee for Energy, According to Experts
entry.summary: Many people rely on coffee to feel alert and energetic, but drinking your cup of joe at these specific times can maximize its energy-boosting benefits.

entry.title: Conan O’Brien reveals overlooked male menopause change that surprised him
entry.summary: The comedian and former late-night host opened up about the moment he realized one of the effects of aging may have been tied to his changing testosterone levels.

entry.title: America at 300: Imagining the next half-century of change
entry.summary: Moon bases, living to 100 and (finally!) flying cars. What 2076 and our future will look like may be stranger than we expect.

entry.title: Column | There’s a new way to treat sprains and strains. Hint: Ice is out.
entry.summary: If you think you need rest, ice and ibuprofen, think again.

entry.title: 8 Fruits That Fight Inflammation and Support Gut Health
entry.summary: Experts break down how powerful antioxidants and fiber can make fruit a great choice for your gut health—and the one tip you need to know to prevent blood sugar spikes.

entry.title: Peptide hype is at an all-time high – I found out the truth
entry.summary: They’re in skincare, lip glosses and weight-loss drugs – but also in ‘grey-market’ injectables where some people are buying products online without sufficient human trials to prove their safety. Charlotte Cripps samples some for herself and learns more about …

entry.title: Scientists find no link between Tylenol and autism, again, after Trump warning
entry.summary: After Trump's claims, Tylenol usage dropped during pregnancies.

entry.title: TikTok’s tick-removal tricks to skip — and how to avoid ‘mouthparts left behind in the skin’
entry.summary: Tick these TikTok tricks off your list.

entry.title: If you’re at risk of Alzheimer’s, these dietary habits can help, study finds
entry.summary: Research suggests diet remains a relevant factor for brain health even after blood markers of dementia are present.

entry.title: New disease threats follow Trump administration's health program cuts
entry.summary: From screwworm to flesh-eating bacteria, mounting public health risks are emerging in the wake of deep cuts to federal health agencies and programs.

entry.title: Miranda Resident Faces Stroke, Cancer Diagnosis — Community Asked to Help
entry.summary: A long-time Southern Humboldt community member is navigating a stroke and an advanced cancer diagnosis at the same time, and friends are asking neighbors to pitch in while he focuses on getting well.

entry.title: Ovaries Appear to Develop an Incredible Second Role After Menopause
entry.summary: We've all heard of menopause: a supposedly terminal moment for the female reproductive system, in which the ovaries stop releasing eggs and presumably call it a day.

entry.title: Iowa State study looks at menopause's impact on the brain
entry.summary: Iowa State University researchers are examining how menopause affects brain and vascular aging to address long-term health risks for women.

entry.title: Hand Dominance Is Driven by Practice, Not Birth
entry.summary: Hand dominance is an emergent byproduct of lifetime tool use rather than an innate trait.

entry.title: Scratching that bug bite might feel good at first but science says it’s a bad idea
entry.summary: Why would something that feels so good be bad?

entry.title: A trip to India left me with 38 parasites in my brain
entry.summary: Lowri's headaches, seizures and psychosis were due to an infection caused by pork tapeworm larvae.

entry.title: Statins: Serious Side Effects on Muscles Are Rare, Study Finds
entry.summary: People who take statins to lower cholesterol are unlikely to develop serious muscular disorders, a new study found. Many people avoid statins out of fear of side effects like muscle aches, but experts say the benefits of the commonly prescribed medication out…

entry.title: One injection reversed osteoarthritis in weeks
entry.summary: A Colorado research team has created experimental osteoarthritis treatments that appear to regenerate damaged joints rather than just relieve pain. In animal studies, a single injection restored arthritic joints to a healthy state within weeks, while a second…

entry.title: West Nile virus detected in Grant County, officials urge community to take caution
entry.summary: West Nile virus has been detected in mosquitoes collected in the Moses Lake area, prompting health officials to urge Grant County residents to take steps to pre

entry.title: Tainted Beef Jerky Caused an Outbreak of Bear Worms
entry.summary: One person was hospitalized with severe symptoms of a Trichinella infection.

entry.title: 11-year-old boy dies of rabies after bat lands on face while he slept
entry.summary: “If you get symptomatic rabies infection, it is near universally fatal. But if you get the prevention before symptoms develop, it is near universally successful.”

entry.title: New colorectal cancer discovery could lead to earlier diagnosis
entry.summary: It’s long been suspected that our gut microbiomes are closely linked to colorectal cancer.

entry.title: People Who Were Stuck In Comas Are Sharing What It Was Actually Like, And I Truly Have No Words
entry.summary: "I was in a medically induced coma for six weeks. There were times I was fully conscious, but I couldn't open my eyes, couldn't move a muscle, and couldn't speak. It was terrifying."

entry.title: She Saved Her Only Birthday Celebration For Her Best Friend—But a Heartless Arrival Left Her Utterly Devastated
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Nutrition apps can help build healthy habits. For some users, their gaming features carry risks
entry.summary: The next time you get a trophy on a smartphone app, it might not be from a game. Gamification has spread into all kinds of online areas. But its spread into nutrition apps has some researchers worried. Health professionals say the apps can be helpful for peop…

entry.title: Scientists discover possible link between 9/11 and accelerated aging
entry.summary: Researchers found that World Trade Center responders with PTSD showed molecular changes associated with faster biological aging and an increased risk of chronic illness.

entry.title: 6 ways to shift your stress mindset to dial down daily anxiety
entry.summary: Stress — and how we think about it — may actually help us thrive.

entry.title: 3 contact lens mistakes that eye doctors wish you would stop making
entry.summary: Contact lenses are safe and effective, but wearing them during some activities can increase the risk of rare but serious complications.

entry.title: 6-Year-Old Girl Bitten by Rabid Bat While Climbing Tree Outside Her Family’s Wisconsin Home
entry.summary: A 6-year-old girl is recovering after being bitten by a rabid bat while climbing a tree outside her family's home in Tigerton, Wisc. During the "terrifying" incident, the animal wrapped its wings around Cece Kale's leg as she screamed for help

entry.title: Don't pressure them: Six science-backed ways to get fussy children to eat vegetables
entry.summary: Small changes in how children experience food everyday can have a lasting impact on what they eat.

entry.title: DR Congo says 1,307 Ebola cases confirmed, including 377 deaths
entry.summary: Outbreak spreads to a fourth province, Haut-Uele, bordering South Sudan and the CAR, according to a media report.

entry.title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's spreads through the brain
entry.summary: A common brain protein may be giving Alzheimer’s disease an unexpected way to spread, carrying toxic Tau proteins from damaged neurons into healthy ones. By blocking these harmful protein packages before they reach new cells, researchers believe it may one da…

entry.title: Summer Skincare Essentials on Amazon
entry.summary: TMZ may collect a share of sales or other compensation from links on this page.

entry.title: We Asked 4 Heart-Health Experts About the Best Way to Lower Blood Pressure—and They All Said the Same Thing
entry.summary: Heart health experts say following the DASH diet is the most powerful lifestyle change to lower your blood pressure or reduce your hypertension risk.

entry.title: Tejano singer warns of West Nile dangers after mosquito bite leaves him in wheelchair
entry.summary: A single mosquito bite two years ago changed Fernando Silva’s life forever and health officials are now warning that this summer’s record mosquito surge across southeast Texas could put thousands of others at risk.

entry.title: ‘Everyday struggle’: Clark County hairstylist recovering after sudden brain bleed during pregnancy
entry.summary: VANCOUVER, Wash. (KOIN) — A Clark County hairstylist is recovering after a sudden medical emergency during pregnancy left her fighting for her life and requiring emergency brain surgery. Makayla Ferdig says she does not remember when the ordeal began. What sh…

entry.title: Rare contagious cancer is tearing through Washington's Puget Sound clams after East Coast jump
entry.summary: Genetic analysis showed that the Puget Sound cancer belongs to the same lineage previously found in Atlantic Coast soft-shell clams.
